content:
# Wavelet Filtration

**Summary**: A multi-frequency process used to denoise signals and reduce data volume while preserving high-frequency features.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2011-alburaikan|AlBuraikan, 2011]] applied wavelet-filtration to manage the large volumes of noisy real-time data from PDHMS.

## Instruments and Software



## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Wavelet filtration can reduce raw data points by 100 to 1,000 times while maintaining critical shut-in information for analysis. ([[2011-alburaikan|AlBuraikan, 2011, p. 37]])
